# utils-merge

[![Build](https://img.shields.io/travis/jaredhanson/utils-merge.svg)](https://travis-ci.org/jaredhanson/utils-merge)
[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/coveralls/jaredhanson/utils-merge.svg)](https://coveralls.io/r/jaredhanson/utils-merge)
[![Quality](https://img.shields.io/codeclimate/github/jaredhanson/utils-merge.svg?label=quality)](https://codeclimate.com/github/jaredhanson/utils-merge)
[![Dependencies](https://img.shields.io/david/jaredhanson/utils-merge.svg)](https://david-dm.org/jaredhanson/utils-merge)


Merge object properties. A lightweight utility function for merging objects, primarily used by Express.js for configuration management and middleware functionality.

## Install

```bash
$ npm install utils-merge
```

## Usage

```javascript
var merge = require('utils-merge');

var a = { foo: 'bar' };
var b = { bar: 'baz' };

merge(a, b);
// => a is now { foo: 'bar', bar: 'baz' }
```

## API

### merge(a, b)

Merges the properties from object `b` into object `a`. The merge is performed in-place, modifying the first argument. The function returns the modified object.

```javascript
var obj = merge({ name: 'John' }, { age: 30 });
// obj is now { name: 'John', age: 30 }
```

Note that this is a shallow merge. Properties from `b` will overwrite properties with the same name in `a`.

```javascript
var obj = merge({ name: 'John' }, { name: 'Jane' });
// obj is now { name: 'Jane' }
```

## Examples

### Merging configuration objects

```javascript
var defaultConfig = {
  host: 'localhost',
  port: 3000,
  timeout: 5000
};

var userConfig = {
  port: 8080,
  debug: true
};

var config = merge(defaultConfig, userConfig);
// config is now { host: 'localhost', port: 8080, timeout: 5000, debug: true }
```

### Use with Express.js

This utility is used internally by Express.js for various operations, such as merging middleware options:

```javascript
var express = require('express');
var app = express();

// Express uses utils-merge internally for operations like this
app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }));
```

## Tests

```bash
$ npm install
$ npm test
```
